description:
You are a platform engineer maintaining a CI/CD pipeline.  
A small Python micro-service that is ready to be published has already been staged for you at  

/home/user/source_release  

The directory currently contains exactly two files:  
1. app.py  
2. requirements.txt  

Your goal is to package this directory, generate an inventory of the archive, and stage an extracted copy for final smoke-tests.  Use **no more than four (4) shell commands** in total.

Exact requirements:

1. Create a compressed tarball called  
   /home/user/package/source_release_v1.tar.gz  
   • The archive must contain the *entire* source_release directory as its top-level entry, preserving the two files and their paths.

2. After the archive is created, produce a plain-text listing of its contents at  
   /home/user/package/source_release_v1.list  
   • The listing file must contain exactly three lines, in this precise order and with the exact spelling and punctuation shown below (each followed by a UNIX newline):  
     source_release/  
     source_release/app.py  
     source_release/requirements.txt  

3. Extract the newly-created archive into the directory  
   /home/user/testing_area  
   so that, after extraction, the following paths exist on disk:  
   /home/user/testing_area/source_release/app.py  
   /home/user/testing_area/source_release/requirements.txt  

You should end with these three artefacts present and correct:
• /home/user/package/source_release_v1.tar.gz  
• /home/user/package/source_release_v1.list (with the three exact lines)  
• /home/user/testing_area/source_release/ (containing the two original files)

No additional files are expected. The automated grader will verify the existence of the tarball, the exact content of the listing file, and the successful extraction of the two files in the testing_area directory.
truth:
Initial filesystem state provided to the agent:

Directory: /home/user/source_release  
├── app.py  
└── requirements.txt  

File contents:

/home/user/source_release/app.py  
--------------------------------  
#!/usr/bin/env python3  
print("Hello, CI/CD!")  

/home/user/source_release/requirements.txt  
------------------------------------------  
requests==2.31.0  

Nothing else exists in /home/user/package or /home/user/testing_area at startup.

Expected final state for verification:

1. /home/user/package/source_release_v1.tar.gz exists and, when listed with  
   tar -tzf /home/user/package/source_release_v1.tar.gz  
   prints exactly:  
   source_release/  
   source_release/app.py  
   source_release/requirements.txt  

2. /home/user/package/source_release_v1.list exists with *byte-exact* contents:  
   source_release/  
   source_release/app.py  
   source_release/requirements.txt  
   (each line terminated with a single line-feed, no carriage returns, no extra blank lines).

3. The following two files exist and their contents are unchanged after extraction:  
   /home/user/testing_area/source_release/app.py     (same 2-line Python script)  
   /home/user/testing_area/source_release/requirements.txt  (single-line requirements file)

Permissions: all new files and directories can be created with the default umask; no root privileges are needed.
